holdings.note: SpaceX — IPO'd Jun 2026; FMP may not yet cover earnings/dividend dates for a brand-new listing.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

INTC.read: INTC is up 10.5% today and 14.4% on the week on below-average volume with no dated company catalyst on the calendar — that's a flow-driven move, not a fundamentals re-rate. With no confirmed earnings date or other binary event to anchor the rally, the asymmetry favors taking some chips off the strength rather than chasing; the only scheduled item ahead is triple witching on 2026-09-18, which is a mechanical unwind risk, not a bull catalyst.
INTC.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (options/futures expiry, mechanical flow event)
INTC.bull: Momentum + short-cover squeeze can extend if sector rotation into laggard semis continues and a foundry/AI headline lands.
INTC.bear: Move is unexplained and on weak volume; gap-fill risk is high once options flow fades, with no earnings catalyst to defend the level.

NVDA.read: No company-specific dated catalyst on the board for NVDA right now — next earnings isn't in the feed, and the only thing ahead is triple witching ~89 days out, which is positioning noise, not a fundamental event. The tape is constructive (+2.8% on the day, +2.7% on the week at $210) so there's no urgency to act either direction. Without a print or guide to lean into, this is a hold-and-wait until an earnings date prints on the calendar.
NVDA.bull: Trend intact, name leading on the week, and no near-term binary risk to navigate.
NVDA.bear: Sept triple witching can amplify mega-cap volatility, and absent a dated earnings anchor you're flying blind on the next real catalyst.

ORCL.read: No company-specific dated catalyst on the board for ORCL right now — price action is flat (1d +0.43%, 5d +0.17%), so there's no stretched move to fade or dislocation to add into. The next real window will be the fiscal Q1 print (typically mid-September), which would also overlap with the 2026-09-18 triple witching — a setup worth pre-positioning for once a confirmed date lands. Until then, this is a hold-and-watch name; let the next earnings date publish before staging any action.
ORCL.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (only dated item on the board; ORCL FQ1 earnings typically prints in this window but is not yet confirmed)
ORCL.bull: OCI/AI-backlog narrative remains the multiple-expansion story; flat tape into a print sets up an asymmetric beat reaction.
ORCL.bear: Stock has re-rated hard on AI capex assumptions — any backlog or margin disappointment into a quad-witching tape gets punished.

NOK.read: NOK is down 2.3% on the day and 4.3% on the week with no dated company catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div in the window provided. Without a near-term event to anchor an add or trim, this is a price-action watch: the question is whether the slide stabilizes near support or extends, not whether to front-run a catalyst. Triple witching on Sep 18 is a tape event, not a NOK-specific one, so it shouldn't drive the decision here.
NOK.bull: Networks order momentum and AI-datacenter optical wins could re-rate the name on the next print.
NOK.bear: No dated catalyst means the weak tape can keep grinding lower with nothing to interrupt it.

NBIS.read: No dated company catalyst on the sheet for NBIS — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div — so there's no real event window to lean into here. The only thing on the calendar is triple witching ~89 days out, which is a market-structure event, not an NBIS-specific catalyst. With a 1-share position and no price-action context, the honest answer is to wait until a confirmed earnings date or company update populates before sizing a stance.
NBIS.bull: AI-infra/neocloud narrative keeps NBIS bid as a high-beta way to play GPU buildout demand.
NBIS.bear: No dated catalyst means the name trades on sentiment and AI-capex headlines — vulnerable to any group de-rating.

KO.read: Tiny 5-share KO position with no dated company catalyst on the board — next earnings and ex-div aren't supplied, and the only thing ahead is triple witching nearly three months out, which is noise for a defensive staple. Down 3.2% on the week without a known driver puts this in "wait for a real catalyst" territory; nothing here forces action on a position this small.
KO.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no KO-specific dated event provided)
KO.bull: Defensive cash-flow compounder pulling back into a quiet tape — weakness without a catalyst is usually a gift in KO.
KO.bear: No dated catalyst means no defined window; the -3.2% week could be the start of a staples derating on rates/FX before any earnings reset.

SPCX.read: SPCX is a brand-new June 2026 IPO with no confirmed earnings or dividend dates in coverage yet, so there's no dated company catalyst to trade against. The only dated event on the horizon is Sept 18 triple witching, which is a market-structure event — relevant for a freshly-listed, lockup-sensitive name where options open interest and dealer positioning can amplify moves, but not a fundamental catalyst. Until the first earnings date gets posted or a lockup expiry is confirmed, the honest move is to let the tape define the range.
SPCX.bull: First post-IPO print and any Starship/Starlink milestone disclosure could anchor a fundamental floor under a name that's still price-discovering.
SPCX.bear: No earnings history, unknown lockup schedule, and a thin float into triple witching is a recipe for outsized two-way moves on flow, not fundamentals.

disclaimer: Forward earnings dates are third-party aggregator estimates, not company-confirmed — confirm against the company's IR release (~2 weeks ahead). Dividends, macro prints and witching are scheduled/confirmed. Not investment advice.
